body.u: I left school at 17 years of age and started work in Abbotts Laboratories as laboratory assistant. I liked my work I enjoyed going out in the evenings with Paul. By then I’d already been going steady with him for a year. When I was 20 I got married to Paul. It was a lovleylovely wedding then sun was shinningshining and everyone enjoyed themselves.

I stayed at work until my first baby was born I was 22 then. She was lovleylovely; she had Auburn hair and lots of it. We called her Hiedi. Three years later I had another baby a boy this time he was really cute. We called him Danny. He turned out to be a right little monkey. Hiedi started school when she was 5 and Danny started 2 years later. I went back to work. The children grew up and the four of us had a marvellous time, especially on our Annual Holidays. We were doing alright financially, Paul had a good job so at 38 I left work and became an ordinary housewife. The next year mum died, it took a long time to get over that as we were very close. Dad moved into our road so that we could keep an eye on him. That year seemed full of deaths. PaulsPaul's Dad died three months afterwards I think it was the shock of his dad dying that caused it. Although the doctors said it was heart trouble I was glad to see that year out. Heidi got married when she was 18 she looked lovely all in white. Danny got married at 20, in the same year Hiedi had her one and only child, Samantha. She had a difficult birth and decided not to have any more. Danny had 3 children the first came two years after his marriage it was a girl called Jayne. The other two came both together, twins, a boy called Simon and a girl called Veronica. I spent nearly all of my spare time with my grandchildren right up until now. I love them dearly and so does Paul, he gets on especially well with Simon, I think it’s because he’s the only boy. He’s a comedian, just like his dad used to be. Every weekend all 10 of us get together at our house. We’re one big happy family who get on just fine. When Paul had a spell in Hospital two months ago all four grandchildgrandchildren came to stay with me, they were good company and cheered me up no end. Anyway Paul’s alright now and we’re all back together again, I hope we will stay like that for a long time, for as long as I live anyway.
